analysis.consistency: The spell description is internally consistent. All defined mechanics (triggering, area of effect, damage scaling, spell storage) are clearly explained.
analysis.quality: High quality. The description thoroughly covers all aspects of casting, inscription choices (Explosive Runes vs. Spell Glyph), targeting, and scaling, which is essential for a complex defensive/trap spell.

mechanics.casting_time: 1 hour (Long setup time reflects its powerful, permanent nature)
mechanics.components: V, S, M (Material component cost of 200 gp, consumed, emphasizes its significant resource investment)
mechanics.duration: Until dispelled or triggered (Permanent effect until triggered or removed, standard for wards)
mechanics.trigger_specificity: Excellent flexibility. Allows precise control over activation based on proximity, interaction, creature type, alignment, or passwords.

explosive_runes.damage: 5d8 initial, scales by +1d8 per spell level above 3rd (e.g., 6d8 at 4th level).
explosive_runes.area: 20-foot-radius sphere, spreads around corners (high potential collateral damage).

spell_glyph.storage_limit: Matches the spell slot level used (e.g., 5th level slot stores a 5th level spell).
spell_glyph.targeting_logic: Stored spell targets the creature that triggered the glyph or centers on it. This handles AOE spells elegantly.
spell_glyph.concentration: Explicitly states that stored spells requiring concentration last for their full duration, preventing exploitation of concentration limits.

mechanics.detection: Requires a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against the caster's spell save DC, making it reasonably hidden but beatable by specialized characters.
